reforms.description: The Norwegian government has significantly increased public expenditure on research and development (R&D) activities, with a rise of around 8 percent in the 1990 National Budget following a previous increase of 22 percent from 1986 to 1989. This initiative aims to promote new industries and enhance the efficiency of existing ones, addressing the relatively low government R&D expenditure compared to other OECD countries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation and efficiency in industries, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in public expenditure on R&D is stated as part of the 1990 National Budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant increase in public R&D funding but does not constitute a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: With a view to promoting new industries and making old activities more efficient, the authorities have strongly stepped up their support for research and development (R&D) activities in the 1980s.

reforms.description: The Norwegian government has announced its participation in a new venture capital company, providing almost half of its capital to support firms facing genuine market failure in funding their start-up operations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting startups through venture capital is expected to foster innovation and economic growth by addressing funding gaps.
reforms.status_evidence: The government decision to participate in the venture capital company is described as an announcement.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative is significant in supporting startups but does not represent a major overhaul of the funding system.
reforms.source_quote: On the recommendation of the Committee, however, the Government decided to participate in a new venture capital company, providing almost half of its capital.

reforms.description: The authorities have implemented measures to ensure that at least 50 percent of R&D expenditures by offshore operators are spent in cooperation with Norwegian contractors, promoting local industry involvement in the offshore oil sector.
reforms.package_name: Local Industry Involvement in Offshore R&D
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging local industry participation in offshore R&D is expected to enhance local economic development and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been described as implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The measures represent a significant effort to integrate local contractors into offshore R&D but do not constitute a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: All production licences since 1979 have required them to ensure that at least 50 per cent of their licence-related expenditures on R&D are spent in co-operation with Norwegian contractors.
